text: Over the last few years, there have been many cases of violent incidents that have resulted in deaths, injuries and threats that have forced Roma to leave their countries of origin in order to seek political asylum in other countries. Recently, this has particularly concerned the Czech Republic and Hungary. Roma have witnessed with their own eyes the destruction of their camps by fire-bombs in Italy, which has obliged them to leave. EU legislation confirms that an asylum seeker can safely be sent back to an EU country considered a "safe country of origin". A citizen of one EU country cannot be given refugee status in another country, except in exceptional cases. The conditions required for a long term residence permit in another EU country are often so difficult to achieve that Roma who have sought political asylum in an EU country find themselves without a future, in a limbo situation and in an irregular situation. The issue of Roma who, fleeing violence in Kosovo some 10 years ago, wish to avoid being sent back to a country that has changed, and in which they fear social exclusion, discrimination and threats to their personal security, cannot be ignored. The chances of their successful integration are slim. In any case, the children who were born in the host countries, or who have lived all their lives there, should not be punished with a return to a country to which they do not feel they belong. 70-75% of Roma returnees have left Kosovo again after a short period. In order to take account of the numerous different aspects of this problem, the rapporteur stresses the need for an individual, specific and fair evaluation of the requests for political asylum made by these people and that they should be able to respond to the sufficient evidence of safe circumstances for asylum applications in one member state as referred to in Article 3, 2 of the Qualification Directive, because they have fled to another member state. The rapporteur calls for a rapid review of the returns policy concerning Roma from Kosovo and asks member states to consider prohibiting the return of Roma to Kosovo and to studying the option of allowing for their integration in the host country. If the returns are nevertheless enforced, these should be carried out under the guarantee of assistance.
